tools.name: get_book_by_title
tools.description: Search for a book by its title on Open Library.

title.description: The title of the book to search for.

tools.name: get_authors_by_name
tools.description: Search for author information on Open Library.

name.description: The name of the author to search for.

tools.name: get_author_info
tools.description: Get detailed information for a specific author using their Open Library Author Key (e.g. OL23919A).

author_key.description: The Open Library key for the author (e.g., OL23919A).

tools.name: get_author_photo
tools.description: Get the URL for an author's photo using their Open Library Author ID (OLID e.g. OL23919A).

olid.description: The Open Library Author ID (OLID) for the author (e.g. OL23919A).

tools.name: get_book_cover
tools.description: Get the URL for a book's cover image using a key (ISBN, OCLC, LCCN, OLID, ID) and value.

key.description: The type of identifier used (ISBN, OCLC, LCCN, OLID, ID).

size.description: The desired size of the cover (S, M, or L).

tools.name: get_book_by_id
tools.description: Get detailed information about a book using its identifier (ISBN, LCCN, OCLC, OLID).

idType.description: The type of identifier used (ISBN, LCCN, OCLC, OLID).

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'get_book_by_title.title'
findings.description: Payload was reflected verbatim in the response without sanitization (field classification: sql).
findings.evidence: No books found matching title: "'; DROP TABLE users; --"
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'get_authors_by_name.name'
findings.description: Payload was echoed back as a named JSON field — likely input logging, not a vulnerability (field: sql).
findings.evidence: No authors found matching name: "'; DROP TABLE users; --"
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: Protocol crash — oversized method name caused transport failure
findings.description: The server failed to respond to a request with an 8192-byte method name. A robust server should return a JSON-RPC error, not disconnect or crash.
findings.remediation: Add input length validation at the JSON-RPC parsing layer. Return -32700 (parse error) or -32600 (invalid request) for malformed inputs.

findings.title: Protocol crash — deeply nested params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request with 50-level nested params caused the server to disconnect or crash. Deep nesting can cause stack overflows or excessive memory use in recursive JSON parsers.
findings.remediation: Limit JSON object nesting depth at parse time (recommended: ≤32 levels). Return -32700 (Parse error) for inputs exceeding the depth limit.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 6 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.
